dialogues.text_en: At last we have a plan. Let's make it count!
According to the records from Yuweyawata, my mother was investigating Origenics for her own research into souls.
There was map data for Origenics in the records, and that's how we learned about the castle in the foundation sector.
The castle was once my home. I shall do my utmost to be of use on this mission.
Calyx determines how many will become Endless, but leaves the choice of who in the hands of the people...
Is this his idea of fairness, or does he simply not care? Perhaps he considers all to be worthy of salvation...? Oh, but it's too early for such speculation...
In the world of Sphene's memories, the town was already submerged. Given their dire circumstances, I expect the people considered stripping the castle for materials to rebuild.
For the structure to endure even now... It speaks to its importance to the Alexandrians, as well as their resilience.
Right. I believe I've plotted a viable route to the foundation sector.
As we don't have much information on the area, I'll come along and gather geographic data on-site. You can leave the navigating to me.
I'll stay behind this time, but let me know if there's anything I can do to help!
Oblivion is fortunate to have a capable adapter like you, Gulool Ja. And no matter what the future holds, your knowledge of electrope is bound to serve you well.
With such dependable support here, we may embark on our mission. Come, let's head to the lift in the government sector.
Be safe out there!
Though it was but from a distance, I remember the sight of Alexandria's castle in the virtual domain. I wonder how it looks now compared to then...
From here, we head to the very bottom of Everkeep. I can't help but feel nervous, returning there after so long...
Calyx has all but invited us, so we must assume that we are expected...
Oblivion's gotten us permission to use the lift. It's nice when things can move quickly.
Everything has been arranged for your passage to the foundation sector!
There's no direct lift to the foundation sector, so we'll have to transfer to another on the way.
If we take the wrong lift, we'll end up in the wrong sector, so be sure to follow her instructions.
According to Teeshal Ja's records, the tower is divided into inner and outer sections below Origenics. The castle is at the heart of the inner section.
So upon arriving at the foundation sector, we need to make our way inwards.
Forename , take this communicator with you.
It's been modified to track your coordinates, so I'll be guiding you as I collect data.
Sounds like we're all set. Let's get going!
It would be nice if this foray was uneventful, but that would be a naive hope. Better to expect the worst.
I shall follow close behind with Shale.
Though we are taking a risk, the far greater danger would be to wait. This time, let us take the battle to Calyx.
Calyx uses the people's love for Sphene to further his own schemes. We can't allow him to do so any longer. Whatever it takes, we'll find and stop him!
We're heading into a trap, so watch your step.
I'll be your guide for the descent to the foundation sector, after which Shale will take over. Please take care not to lose the communicator!
There are beasts ahead of you. They must have gotten in and made themselves at home.
There should be an access hatch nearby that leads inside. Look for an opening.
I'm detecting something big... You'll have to deal with it to continue on.
Good work. Now, proceed to the access hatch and head inside.
Everkeep's construction was apparently a constant battle against the rising water level.
Even during the war, the people labored to maintain the castle. It meant a lot to them.
It's upsetting to think that Preservation's terminal is in this place...
Hold on... Someone's jam...mmunications! Stay alert!
So you have come. Let us measure your strength.
I am the soldier S0, of Alexandria's castle-guard. By order of the royal knights, I shall dispatch all intruders.
Releasing limiters... Initiating mobile combat maneuvers.
Calling reinforcements. Together we shall triumph.
Combat functions...inoperable... Forgive my failure...
That was hardly a challenge, I see.
You will show me more. I bid you press on.
You are untroubled thus far, but I have something that may test you yet.
We still lack sufficient data. Very well. Let us see how you perform against this.
This machine has long guarded these crumbling halls.
How will you handle this?
Let us introduce more weaponry.
Let us see what this yields.
Perhaps a new approach to assess your adaptability.
Still alive. How unfortunate.
Were you to die here, it would save me trouble later.
I'm beginning to understand how you were able to defeat Queen Eternal.
That will do. We have enough to proceed with an analysis.
Shale and Sphene shouldn't be far behind.
And so the trap was sprung, as expected.
Where are you hiding, Calyx!?
It seems we must clear a path through these creatures.
Indeed. Let us proceed with caution.
Let us press on.
Now to put my time training on the beach to good use!
The sand has built up over the long years...
Whoa! That's a big bug!
Has it been using this chamber as its lair...?
Huh? Where's it going?
Is it moving along the pipes?
I don't think it's trying to flee...
where did it hide!?
On your guard, everyone!
It means to catch us by surprise!
Here it comes again!
What a tiresome foe...
This time... From over there!
Hmph. What a vexing creature that was.
To think it was clever enough to use the pipes and sand to its advantage...
Wait, is that...the castle!?
Look. Could that be...?
...It's the castle!?
Was Shale's transmission cut off!?
We knew it wouldn't be easy. Let's stay strong and keep going!
That voice... Calyx!?
Is he testing us against that mechanical soldier?
What!? Copies!?
It moves so quickly...
What are we up against now?
We won't be tricked again!
Remain calm—we can evade its attacks!
Focus and follow the movements!
Even more of them!?
Ungh... My head is spinning.
No doubt it will fight to the last...
It was following orders... Does an even fiercer machine await us?
Fine by me. If he wants a show, let's give him one!
Gah! They came out of nowhere!
What a fright!
Is it guarding the door?
All the more reason to see just what lies beyond.
I don't believe it intends to let us pass...
What is that? A floating rock?
Something's carved into it... Alexandria's emblem, perhaps?
Stay alert!
Those things started moving!
It comes again!
Is anywhere safe!?
Something's different...
Is the lightning intensifying?
Steel your nerves and make ready to dodge!
The red light changed positions!
Those move too!? No fair!
Calyx was measuring our strength. Is that why he contrived to lead us here?
It looks like we've cleared away the machines and beasts, but who knows what else he might have in store...
From here on out, Sphene and Shale will move with us. Let's find that terminal, but stay alert for more threats.
Before my illness left me bedridden, I used to walk through here on my way to watch Otis and Zelenia train.
You knew Otis, if I am not mistaken? Zelenia was his second-in-command in the royal knights of Alexandria.
Oh, how I loved to see the two of them spar. I once dreamed of fighting by their side, so I studied the magical arts under my tutor.
But when I was fifteen, the Storm Surge broke out, and both my father and mother passed away.
As the new queen, I was forbidden from taking to the battlefield, for our nation would fracture had I died without a successor.
Though in the end I could barely move, I regret that I didn't go out there and fight. At least while I still could.
My Endless self lamented not being able to protect the realm with benevolence. 'Tis a feeling to which I can relate...
Sphene...
Ah, forgive me! 'Twas not my intent to darken the mood. Come, let us press on.
This is one big castle, though... How far does this hallway go?
Do you know, Sphene?
What...?
What...what happened!?
Oh? It seems our guest brought company.
So this was your doing.
It was. We installed a teleporter that is triggered by the presence of the key, you see.
Ah, I'm afraid aetheric transmissions cannot penetrate the walls of the throne room. It will take your friends a good while to find you.
I confess, the Valia Pira did not suffice to assess your capabilities.
So I shall try something else.
You are free to use anything at your disposal. The better you fight, the more accurate my data.
Attaining a precise measure of your strength─that is the most expeditious way...
...To reclaim the key to which you so stubbornly cling.
Ah, noble Zelenia, dashing as ever. These villains mean to do us harm. Strike them down, I bid you!
What? This machine is Zelenia!?
Indeed it is. We couldn't preserve her memories while she lived, so she has been reconstructed from our recollections of her.
The Endless me being created in answer to the earnest desires of Alexandrians... That, I can accept.
But to twist the memories of those I held dear and treat them as playthings...
...This disgrace I cannot abide!
Loath though I am to comply with their demands, this monstrosity must be destroyed. Not least of all for Zelenia's honor.
If she was indeed created from my memories, even if I cannot fight, I may be able to offer you counsel.
So please, lend me your strength and end this farce!
Ah, there it is...
I am Zelenia Triantafylli.
Royal knight of Alexandria, and sworn blade of Her Majesty, the Queen.
Upon my blade I swear, no harm shall come to Her Highness!
You will not defile Zelenia's memory any longer!
Feel the sting of my divine blade!
I know this technique... You must keep moving!
Have at you!
Fall!
That her bladework should be recreated so faithfully...
I will suffer none to threaten Alexandria!
Heavens roar!
Have care! Zelenia was capable of much, much more!
I will show you the soul of a royal knight!
For the honor and glory of Alexandria, I strike!
No more games!
Quickly, away from the sigils!
The sigils channel magic! Be on your guard!
You've no time even to fear my wrath!
You must brace for blade and magic both!
Taste my steel!
There is no escape!
Surge!
I know this is merely a reconstruction...and yet...
I have pledged my life to the kingdom!
My resolve shall never waver!
I am sworn to protect my queen! I will die before I yield!
That's right... Zelenia was always there to protect me...
Rage and roil!
A disgraceful display... Forgive me, my queen... I have failed you...
Thank you for keeping me from harm.
Did you get what you needed?
I did. Though these figures will complicate matters.
Is that so? Well, if we are done here, then I shall end the transmission.
Hold it!
If you have no scruples about thus defiling the memories of Zelenia...then there is no way we will ever see eye to eye.
You are not fit to be entrusted with the cherished memories of Alexandrians!
I, for one, have no wish to employ the fruits of my research in this manner. It is a waste of time and effort.
But since I relinquished my flesh long ago, I must resort to every conceivable method, so long as the hero insists on keeping the key.
Why do you so oppose our objective?
With the key, we would be able to harvest aether from other reflections and bring forth more Endless.
We could give this chance to evolve not only to the people of the Ninth, but to those of the Source as well. I see no reason why you should reject such a boon.
How miserly you are! But unless you're willing to compromise, you won't be able to save anyone or anything.
Oh, don't tell me you thought you were the only ones who knew? That is the height of hubris!
Well, I'd say our conversation has run its course.
Ultimately, what you think is immaterial, for the seeds we've sown have already quickened.
The people have glimpsed hope in transcending death, and they can ill turn away from it.
And thus does my role come to an end. The rest, I leave in your capable hands, Calyx.
As a token of gratitude for consenting to my tests, I leave you with a gift. Be sure to take it with you.
Heading below? I'd be glad to show you the way!
Zelenia was kind, but she could be stern. A pillar of strength when I ascended the throne in troubled times.
Not only did she protect me, she also buoyed my spirit whenever it threatened to buckle under the pressures of my station...
By preserving her memory and honor, you've done more for me than you could know. Thank you, Forename .
